insight: For Türkiye, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have been a significant factor in gaining momentum, with the team successfully doing so in both of their breaks, resulting in a mean increase of 0.100 points per minute. This suggests that the breaks have given Türkiye a chance to regroup and refocus, leading to improved performance on the field. Interestingly, when Türkiye was pressing before a break, they maintained their momentum but didn't gain any additional advantage, whereas when they weren't pressing, they were able to gain more momentum after the break, with an average increase of 0.200 points per minute. Overall, the data indicates that hydration breaks have been a positive turning point for Türkiye, allowing them to adjust their strategy and come back stronger.
